text.title: Jenkins Basics Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the jenkins-basics methodology end-to-end.

step.name: confirm-jenkins-fits
step.input: Project host (GitHub / GitLab / self-hosted), team size, compliance needs.
step.action: Apply the decision tree (06-decision-tree.xml). If host=GitHub and no compliance lock-in, STOP and use github-actions-basics instead. If host=GitLab, STOP and use gitlab-cicd.
step.decision-gate: If decision != jenkins, abandon this methodology.

step.name: select-agent-strategy
step.action: If a Kubernetes cluster is reachable, pick `agent { kubernetes { yaml '...' } }` with explicit resource requests/limits. Otherwise pick `agent { docker { image '...' } }` for single-container builds. Forbid `agent any`.
step.output: Agent block + pod-template YAML if K8s.

step.name: write-jenkinsfile
step.input: Build/test/deploy commands + branch policy + secret inventory.
step.action: Write Declarative Jenkinsfile: pipeline → agent → options(timeout, buildDiscarder, disableConcurrentBuilds) → stages(Install/Validate/Test/Build/Deploy) → post(always cleanWs, failure slackSend). Inject every secret via `withCredentials`.

step.name: capture-controller-config
step.action: Export running config to `jcasc/jenkins.yaml` (Manage Jenkins → Configuration as Code → View). Lock plugins via `plugins.txt`. Set controller executors to 0. Commit both files to source control.
step.decision-gate: If JCasC plugin is not installed, install it before continuing.

step.name: validate-and-register
step.action: Run `validate-jenkins-basics.py --file artefact.json`. Submit the Jenkinsfile via the Pipeline Linter REST endpoint (`POST /pipeline-model-converter/validate`). Register the repo as a Multibranch Pipeline (single repo) or under an Organization Folder (whole org).
